skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: 3-D physical entity conveying information, e.g. a model, artefact or architectural feature.
A written work being one of several found in a monograph or serial
Moving images made available through projection, video or other methods.
A written work issued at regular intervals and numbered consecutively.
Measurements or factual information which may occur in a manual catalogue system or database.
Documents relating to the planning, inception and management of a project.
Photographic print on self-developing photographic paper in a format developed by Polaroid in the 1970's
Unpublished written work such as manuscripts, dissertations etc. not disseminated to the public.
An article, but published as a separate item.
Not subdivided
Includes interim, research, specialist reports on a specific topic.

skos:Concept.skos:altLabel: National Mapping Programme Morph Number Sheet

skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: Not subdivided
Maps or charts.
Use for completed, accuarately surveyed maps
Photographic plate, on glass, developed using a wet process.
Photographic negative on film. Use for all acetate material types.
Photographic negative developed on paper.
Photographic negative using nitrate process on a film base material.
Photographic negative on glass developed using a dry process.
Photographic print on glass, using a wet process.
Photographic negative developed on waxed paper.
Photographic negative on polyester base material.
Pictorial, painted or drawn material such as slides, drawings, photos, plans excluding cartographic.
?what does this mean?
A sheet of flat film, 105 x 148mm in size, carrying a matrix of micro images.
Photographic print using silver gelatine.
NB currently Includes ISOMETRIC though this has a separate term.
Photographic transparency on an acetate base material.
Photographic image produced on transparent medium suitable for use in projection equipment. Use specific term where known.
A recording of voice, music or other sounds.
Includes excavation, research and general notes.
Daily record of events or appointments.
Photographic image with normal polarity.
Photographic colour print developed using wet process.
A complete written work including books and anthologies.
Includes individual index cards
Colour positive print developed using a dry process.
Lantern slide developed using a dry process.
Lantern slide developed using a wet process.
Forms used in the recording of archaeological or other site investigations.
Photographic print using carbon
Photographic transparency on nitrate base material.
Reproductive process for non-photographic media in which the image is created by transferring ink from a plate, block etc.
Record of an individual context (ie layer or feature) from an archaeological excavation.
Particulars of a property for sale.
Includes dissertations
Photographic format.
Photographic transparency mounted for use in projection equipment.
Mechanical reproductive process using photographic, electrostatic and dry powder techniques.
Photomechanical or photolithographical reproduction process representing a photographic image as a series of dots or lines.
Photographic image produced using an ink-jet printer.
Record of events.
Early form of photographic print.
Photographic print using albumen.
Loose notes
Use a term from the Archive Format list.
Report prepared by a specialist in a particular field, often as a contribution to a wider project.
Record colour in appropriate field.
